div1.entryFree: Thump, vb. to beat with something blunt, to cuff (cf. Bethump): jump her and t. her, Wint. IV, 4, 196. see thou t. thy master well, H6B II, 3, 85. whom our fathers have beaten, bobbed and --ed, R3 V, 3, 334. thus I t. it (my heart) down, Tit. III, 2, 11. Applied to the stroke of a bird-bolt: proceed, sweet Cupid: thou hast --ed him with thy bird-bolt under the left pap, LLL IV, 3, 24. And used as an interjection: I shoot thee at the swain. T. then and I flee, LLL III, 66.
